description_long: This is a small wooden cabin with a prominent gable roof featuring multiple horizontal ridges, giving it a layered appearance. The walls are constructed from interlocking logs or planks, arranged in a pattern that suggests traditional log cabin joinery. The front side has a central doorway, but no visible windows, emphasizing a simple and utilitarian design. The roof overhangs slightly on all sides, providing some protection from the elements. The wood appears to be stained a medium brown, with a matte finish that highlights the natural grain. The overall structure is compact and sturdy, suitable for use as a rustic shelter or small residential building.
description: A compact wooden cabin with a layered gable roof and interlocking log walls, featuring a central doorway and no visible windows. The structure is sturdy, with a natural brown wood finish.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the layered gable roof and the rectangular footprint of the cabin.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the central doorway and the triangular gable roof, with no windows visible.
description_view_2: Rear or opposite side view highlighting the roof's horizontal ridges and the log wall pattern, with no openings.
description_view_3: Side view showing the solid triangular gable end and the absence of windows or doors.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with multiple horizontal ridges and moderate overhangs, constructed from wood
attribute_window_arrangement: No windows are visible on any side of the cabin
attribute_door_placement: Single exterior door centered on the front facade beneath the gable
